rulings.Case Title: BARBARA WILLIAMS VS. JULIE YEE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 3, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANTS JULIE YEE AND DAVID YEE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT: The demurrer is overruled; 10 days to answer. Plaintiff has alleged a continuing nuisance within the meaning of Baker v. Burbank-Glendale-Pasadena Airport (1985) 39 Cal.3d 862, 868-872. Baker directs the trial courts not to expand the concept of a permanent nuisance. Here, the closest analogous decision is Tracy v. Ferrera (1956) 144 Cal.App.2d 156, 158 cited with approval in Baker. Moreover, the Court in Baker made clear that if there is any ambiguity in the nature of the nuisance, plaintiff may elect. It is true that the 1993 construction on defendant's property is the source of the nuisance; however, the nuisance results from the periodic flow of water and plaintiff has alleged that the nuisance can be cured. This also is a factor in determining whether a nuisance is permanent or continuing. Phillips v. City of Pasadena (1945) 27 Cal.2d 104, 107-108. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MICHELL ENGINEERING, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 3, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANT MITCHELL ENGINEERING'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT HAS DEMONSTRATED THAT THE ALAMEDA ACTION INVOLVES THE SAME CLAIMS AND PARTIES AND WAS FILED IN ALAMEDA BEFORE THE CITY FILED THIS ACTION. THE CITY'S CLAIMS IN THIS CASE CONSTITUTE A MANDATORY CROSS-COMPLAINT WHICH MUST BE FILED IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S ALAMEDA COMPLAINT. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE APPEARS TO BE UNNECESSARY. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: 951-957 MISSION STREET ASSOCIATES,L.P. VS. STEPHEN CARRIGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Quash Service Of Process And For Dismissal (Ccp 418.10, 1167.4) Of Action, Request To Take Judical Ntc
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 3, 2008 LINE 16. DEFENDANT ALTERNATIVE PATIENT CAREGIVERS COLLECTIVE, L.L.C.'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Process And For Dismissal (C.C.P. SECTION 418.10, 1167.4) Of Action: IS DENIED. AS A SUB-TENANT, PLAINTIFF WAS NOT REQUIRED TO SERVE THE MOVING PARTY WITH THE THIRTY-DAY NOTICE. AS A CONSEQUENCE, DEFENDANT HAS NO STANDING TO CHALLENGE THE ADEQUACY OF THE NOTICE. THE COURT GRANTS THE PARTIES' REQUEST TO CONSOLIDATE THESE CASES (624260, 624261, 624262, 624263, 624264 AND 624265) FOR PRE-TRIAL AND TRIAL. CASE NUMBER 624260 IS DESIGNATED AS THE LEAD CASE. =(302/PJM/VC)
